hyperedges.label: Bluetooth laser measurement pipeline (capture to quote)

nodes.label: Fail-Closed Override Policy
nodes.rationale: Only an explicit Admin (RBAC OVERRIDE_AI_DECISION, owner-only) override with a >=20-char written justification can unlock a held case; the gate uses RBAC authority, not a client-supplied role, so it cannot be spoofed; an override only unlocks the Send button and never auto-resends — recordSent() is a separate, explicitly-human, separately-audited action.

nodes.label: Hash-Chained Immutable Audit Trail
nodes.rationale: Append-only, deep-frozen, FNV-1a + SHA-256 chained, optionally HMAC-signed entries make tampering detectable at the exact index; multi-writer lanes keyed by writerId let concurrent devices append without collisions while keeping each lane independently verifiable.

nodes.label: Override Drift Detection (pattern alerts)
nodes.rationale: Repeated overrides of the same category beyond PATTERN_THRESHOLD signal model drift or an overly aggressive classifier; threshold windowing plus cooldown ensures escalation fires only on a crossing, never on every override, so the owner is alerted without spam.

nodes.label: AAA HyperKernel (operational intelligence layer)
nodes.rationale: The kernel is industry-agnostic: carpet-business domain lives in data and contracts, never in the kernel, so the same kernel can run the next physical business unchanged. No agent ever calls another agent; the only legal path is Agent -> Event -> Graph -> Agent.

nodes.label: 25-Event Business Taxonomy
nodes.rationale: Each event is a registered, schema-validated, versioned contract classified on five orthogonal axes (domain, stage, primitive, reversibility, risk) so generic machinery can reason about events it has never seen; invalid payloads are rejected before logging so the log only ever contains well-formed truth.

nodes.label: Dual Event Bus (synchronous reactions + durable hash-chained truth)
nodes.rationale: Debate A: two buses risk drift, but the durable bus bridges selected AAA_EVENTS topics automatically (Bus.bridge()) so real activity is captured without editing emitters; a bridged mirror that fails its contract is dropped, never half-written. The synchronous bus is for reactions; the durable bus is the truth.
nodes.norm_label: dual event bus (synchronous reactions + durable hash-chained truth)

nodes.label: Derived Knowledge Graph (projection, not graph database)
nodes.rationale: Debate B: a graph projected on read from the event log + entity store can never disagree with source records because it IS them; it is offline-first, zero-infrastructure, and makes 'rebuild the world at time T' trivial by replay. Correctness beats convenience; a graph DB remains an optional projection target, never the system of record.
nodes.norm_label: derived knowledge graph (projection, not graph database)

nodes.label: Autonomy Bounded by Reversibility
nodes.rationale: Debate C: 'high confidence' is the model's self-report and can be miscalibrated, so autonomy is bounded by reversibility, not confidence. The action-safety gate classifies allow/needs_approval/deny by blast radius; only local, reversible, internal actions may auto-run; catastrophic actions never run; everything else waits for a human with a reason.

nodes.label: Five Closed Primitives (Entity, Relationship, Event, Decision, Memory)
nodes.rationale: Debate D rejected a sixth Policy primitive because Policy = Entity + Decision; every new primitive multiplies the surface every subsystem must handle, so the five are closed and a sixth requires a recorded debate proving irreducibility.
nodes.norm_label: five closed primitives (entity, relationship, event, decision, memory)

nodes.label: Honest by Construction
nodes.rationale: Debate E: a heuristic dressed as analysis is a lie the operator will trust, and fabricated learning signal poisons training data permanently. With no model proxy configured the system returns AI_NOT_CONFIGURED and falls back to real deterministic data, clearly labeled — an empty truth beats a full fiction.

nodes.label: First-Class Provenance (five answerable questions)
nodes.rationale: Every recommendation must answer: what created it, why, what information was available, who approved it, what happened afterward. Traces are immutable and append-only so 'what did the system know at the time?' is always answerable after the fact.

nodes.label: Learning Closure Loops (estimate, recommendation, prediction)
nodes.rationale: No learning signal is discarded: every estimate resolves to won/lost/expired, every recommendation to validated/invalidated, every prediction is scored against reality.

nodes.label: Council Structure (adversarial debate venues)
nodes.rationale: A council is a debate venue with a charter and roster, not a mailbox — members do not message each other; they each read the graph and vote, and a supervisor arbitrates. A recommendation is accepted because it survived Critic -> Risk -> Supervisor challenge, not because it sounded good.

nodes.label: Agent Lifecycle (define to evolve/retire under contract)
nodes.rationale: An agent is a persona under contract, not free-floating code; prompt and model changes are governed artifacts (versioned, reviewed, recorded) so 'which prompt/model was in force when this decision was made?' is always answerable.
nodes.norm_label: agent lifecycle (define to evolve/retire under contract)

nodes.label: Memory Lifecycle (capture, normalize, index, recall, score, decay)
nodes.rationale: Thin-sample memory yields null confidence (unproven, not wrong) until it earns weight; minimum-sample thresholds prevent one anecdote from becoming a pattern. Memory is owner- and workspace-scoped, additive, and reproducible via a deterministic offline embedder.
nodes.norm_label: memory lifecycle (capture, normalize, index, recall, score, decay)

nodes.label: Dual-Ledger Audit Architecture
nodes.rationale: Two independent hash-chained ledgers (event log with seq+prevHash+cyrb53; governance ledger with FNV-1a + SHA-256, deep-frozen) plus append-only provenance give a tamper-evident spine; auditability is the property that makes autonomy safe.

nodes.label: Kernel Invariants (10 rules every change must preserve)
nodes.rationale: A PR that violates an invariant is wrong by definition regardless of what it enables: only Agent->Event->Graph->Agent paths, five closed primitives, no fake success, no silent governance override, no discarded learning signal, state derived from the immutable log, honest by construction, industry-agnostic kernel, humans final authority.
nodes.norm_label: kernel invariants (10 rules every change must preserve)

nodes.label: Six-Layer Intelligence Pipeline
nodes.rationale: No analysis is accepted without passing all six layers: deterministic data collection (no model), analysis grounded only in Layer-1 numbers, adversarial validation, calibrated supervisor review, executive rollup, and learning/evolution — analysts are forbidden from inventing figures.

nodes.label: Bluetooth Laser Measurement Layer
nodes.rationale: Implemented with the Web Bluetooth API because the app is a vanilla-JS PWA, not React Native/Expo; iOS does not support Web Bluetooth so the app detects this and routes to manual entry. Safety guarantees by design: never blocks a quote, never finalizes price (everything is needsReview), never loses field data (local save before network), never fabricates (unparseable readings return null), workspace-isolated.

nodes.label: Huepar S60 Provisional Protocol (experimental/huepar-s60-v1)
nodes.rationale: The GATT contract comes from public reverse-engineering of the LDM-S60-BT, not vendor certification, so it is deliberately marked experimental and must be lab-validated before being trusted for money-bearing measurements. Metres are canonical; implausible decodes are rejected rather than guessed; an in-band quality flag drops confidence so low-trust reads land in review; angle/tilt frames are diagnostics only; the checksum is recorded but never gated on because the algorithm is unverified.

nodes.label: Governed Private GPU Model Provider (P24)
nodes.rationale: The app never sees the GPU URL or key — it POSTs to a same-origin proxy that holds PRIVATE_GPU_MODEL_URL/KEY; port 8000 is not public and must be locked to the backend egress IP (the API key is defense in depth, not the perimeter). With nothing configured the proxy returns GPU_NOT_CONFIGURED and the app falls back rather than fabricating output; the model is off by default and owner-enabled via Model Governance.

nodes.label: Server-Side Claude Proxy (Firebase claudeProxy / Supabase claude-proxy)
nodes.rationale: All model calls go through a server-side proxy so the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY never lives in the browser; the web/anon keys are safe client-side only because Firestore security rules / Postgres RLS control access.
nodes.norm_label: server-side claude proxy (firebase claudeproxy / supabase claude-proxy)

nodes.label: NVIDIA Nemotron Provider (hosted, OpenAI-compatible)
nodes.rationale: Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B is a 30B model needing a GPU host, so NVIDIA's hosted endpoint is used instead of local serving to keep the app a PWA + serverless deploy; switching provider is a config flip (aiProvider:'nemotron') with no agent code changes because the translate layer maps agent-pinned Claude model ids to the Nemotron model.

nodes.label: Fail-Closed Review-Safety Verdict Mapping
nodes.rationale: Only a clean 'safe' verdict allows a normal send; 'unsafe' blocks, and unknown/unreadable/proxy-error/guardrail-unavailable all queue for human review rather than defaulting to safe. The guardrail only classifies — it never blocks, edits, sends or stores; 'safe' is null (unknown) rather than a false 'safe' when a reply cannot be read, so callers can fail closed. Scoped intentionally to AI-drafted review texts; the human-authored template fallback is not screened.

nodes.label: Receipt Intelligence Pipeline (capture, OCR, classify, human review, post)
nodes.rationale: Nothing posts to the books without a human: REVIEW_RECEIPTS is human-only in the gateway and an origin:'ai' call is hard-blocked and audited. Posting is idempotent (a receipt becomes at most one expense); low-confidence OCR lands in needs_review and unknown vendors get Uncategorized + low confidence instead of guesses; duplicates cannot post without explicit override; financial collections are owner-only; every human re-categorization teaches the classifier.
nodes.norm_label: receipt intelligence pipeline (capture, ocr, classify, human review, post)

nodes.label: Supabase Backend Setup (shared memory + AI proxy)
nodes.norm_label: supabase backend setup (shared memory + ai proxy)

nodes.label: Legal Intelligence Division (CLIO agent org)
nodes.rationale: Not legal advice: the division is advisory and documentation only — it identifies risk, preserves evidence, and recommends human attorney review; the guardrail is a hard constant in every legal agent prompt. It is built on top of the existing kernel (Runtime Gateway, RBAC, Knowledge Graph, Agent OS, Event bus) rather than as a parallel silo, which is what makes it production-grade. No legal agent can finalize a binding action; humans approve through the Gateway.

nodes.label: legal_records Versioned Append-Only Memory
nodes.rationale: Nothing is silently modified — every revision increments version and pushes the prior snapshot into history; every item carries source, timestamp, author, confidence, risk, and audit trail.

nodes.label: Token-as-Credential Portal Security Model
nodes.rationale: An unguessable token in the share link is the only credential, mapping to one contract in one workspace, with server-side expiry and revocation checks; responses are built by whitelist so internal financials (labor/material cost, margins, internal notes) never leave the server even though the Admin SDK can read everything; the only mutation is the customer signing their own contract, and every view/sign is appended to the audit_log.

nodes.label: Server-Side OAuth Secret Custody
nodes.rationale: QuickBooks OAuth2 requires a client secret that cannot live in a PWA, so the Cloud Function exchanges and stores tokens server-side in Firestore (never returned to the client), verifies Firebase ID token + workspace membership (owner role for sensitive actions), audits every attempt, retries transient errors, and only ever creates invoices additively when approved:true — it never updates, voids, or deletes.

nodes.label: Research Brain (/api/research proxy)
nodes.rationale: A read-only strategy advisor: it never touches jobs, quotes, invoices, payments, bookkeeping, or customer records — enforced in code (research-brain.js writes only to research_reports) and asserted by tests. AI-Q must stay read-only until independently security-reviewed before any write path is granted. Until AIQ_RESEARCH_URL is set the proxy returns RESEARCH_NOT_CONFIGURED rather than fabricating reports.

nodes.label: NVIDIA AI-Q Research Service (separate host)
nodes.rationale: AI-Q needs Python, GPUs/NIM, and an NVIDIA_API_KEY, so it cannot run inside the static PWA; it runs on its own host behind HTTPS and a bearer token and the app only calls it over HTTPS.

nodes.label: OpenAI Whisper (transcription provider)
nodes.rationale: Confidence (0-100) is derived from Whisper verbose_json per-segment avg_logprob, duration-weighted; responses with no segments report 0 (unknown) rather than a fabricated number. Without OPENAI_API_KEY the function returns MISSING_API_KEY and the client keeps the audio note for later retry — transcription is additive, never required.

nodes.label: Zero-Dependency Test Harness (npm test)
nodes.rationale: A committed, zero-dependency harness gates every PR on real tests, not just 'it builds'; each suite runs in its own process so globals don't leak; the nested test/package.json forces CommonJS so suites can require() the browser-global app sources; the boot smoke test loads every index.html script in order to catch load-order bugs.

nodes.label: Firestore Rules Emulator Tests
nodes.rationale: Prove the security rules actually enforce RBAC + isolation: workspace isolation, crew cannot read financial collections, audit_log is append-only + owner-read, member docs are not client-writable, and integrations/** OAuth tokens are fully denied to clients. They require the Firebase emulator (Java), so they run in a dedicated CI job.

nodes.label: PWA Icon 192x192 (fully transparent placeholder, no artwork)
nodes.norm_label: pwa icon 192x192 (fully transparent placeholder, no artwork)

nodes.label: White glyph on transparent background (exact shape indiscernible against white viewer background)
nodes.norm_label: white glyph on transparent background (exact shape indiscernible against white viewer background)
